identificationModule.briefTitle: Nivolumab in Combination With Temozolomide and Radiotherapy in Children and Adolescents With Newly Diagnosed High-grade Glioma
identificationModule.officialTitle: Phase I-II Study of Nivolumab in Combination With Temozolomide and Radiotherapy in Children and Adolescents With Newly Diagnosed High-grade Glioma

leadSponsor.name: Gustave Roussy, Cancer Campus, Grand Paris

conditions: High Grade Glioma

interventions.name: Nivolumab
interventions.description:
Solution for intravenous injection 10 mg/ml. Initial dose : 3 mg/kg Nivolumab will be given at 3 mg/kg/injection every two weeks from the first day of radiotherapy to the last day of chemotherapy.

One de-escalation dose : 1 mg/kg

interventions.name: Temozolomide
interventions.description: Capsules: 5, 20, 100, 140, 180 and 250 mg orally. Temozolomide will be given at 75mg/m2/day from the day of start of radiotherapy to the last day of radiotherapy, then, after one month rest at 200mg/m2/day for five consecutive days for 12 cycles (28 days cycle).

interventions.name: Radiotherapy
interventions.description: Total dose of 54 Gray(Gy) units delivered in 30 daily fractions of 1.8 Gy over 6 weeks during the chemoradiation period.
